Based on your recent transactions, you've spent $559.46 at Costco in the last 30 days—$501.23 on groceries and $58.23 on gas. Your current spending pattern suggests you're a "power shopper," and there are two primary ways you could potentially optimize these expenses to put more money back in your pocket. ✨
Membership tier optimization
Your recent monthly spend of $559.46 scales to approximately $6,713 annually. For most shoppers, the break-even point to justify the $120 Executive membership over the $60 Gold Star tier is about $3,000 in qualifying annual purchases.
Estimated annual rewards: At your current pace, the 2% Executive reward would earn you approximately $134.27 per year.
Net benefit: Since the upgrade costs an additional $60, you would effectively "profit" $74.27 annually just by switching tiers, assuming your spending remains consistent.
Keep in mind that while gas purchases don't earn the 2% Executive reward, your grocery and household spending alone are already well above the threshold where the upgrade pays for itself.
Payment strategy and credit rewards
I noticed you're currently using your Chase Sapphire Preferred card for your Costco trips. While that’s an excellent card for travel and dining, Costco only codes as a "warehouse club," meaning you're likely only earning 1 point per dollar on those purchases.
Costco Anywhere Visa strategy: This card offers 2% back on all Costco purchases and 4% back on gas (on the first $7,000 per year).
The "stack" effect: If you combined the Executive membership with the Costco Visa, you would earn a total of 4% back on your warehouse purchases (2% from membership + 2% from the card).
Comparison: On your estimated $6,713 annual spend, the Costco Visa + Executive stack would yield approximately $268.54 in total annual rewards, compared to roughly $67.14 in value if you continue using your current 1% setup.
Maximizing the "Kirkland" advantage
Given your $501 grocery spend this month, you may want to audit how much of your basket is "Kirkland Signature." Market data for 2026 indicates that Costco’s private label items are typically 20% to 25% cheaper than national brands at stores like Walmart or Safeway, while often matching or exceeding the quality of premium brands. Specifically for your Seattle-area household, bulk-buying staples like coffee, nuts, and organic meats at Costco consistently offers the lowest per-unit cost in the region.
Note that account balances from aggregators (such as Plaid) may be delayed or incomplete. Before making a transfer or payment, please verify the exact available balance directly in your bank account. If the balance is different, share the updated amount here and I can refine the recommendation.
